cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure out how long the experiment lasted based on the text. First, I'll look through the text for clues about the duration of the experiment. The passage mentions that participants were assigned to write for 20 minutes per day for three consecutive days. So the writing phase of the experiment lasted three days. Then, it says that "two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a standard 4mm skin biopsy on their inner arm." So there's a two-week gap between the end of the writing phase and the biopsy. The wounds were photographed regularly over the following days to determine the healing rate. The text then states that "on the 11th day after the biopsy, the wounds completely healed on 76.2 percent of those who had done the expressive writing." So 11 days after the biopsy, they looked at the healing rate. Let me add up the time. The writing phase was 3 days. Then two weeks (14 days) later they did the biopsy. Then they observed for 11 days. So the total time from the start of the experiment (the first day of writing) to the end of the observation period (the 11th day after the biopsy) would be 3 days (writing) + 14 days (waiting for the biopsy) + 11 days (observation) = 28 days. 28 days is roughly a month.
